findings.rationale: decoded user-controlled URL parts re-encoded without explicit allow-list
findings.explanation: canonicalizeURLPart blindly decodeURIComponent's the input then re-encodes. If a malicious pathname part contains a UTF-8 sequence that survives decode but produces unexpected characters after re-encoding, the resulting segment could mismatch server-side routing in ways that bypass middleware checks.

findings.rationale: context-line lookup adjacent to changed code
findings.explanation: The empty-string fallback at this control-flow branch silently swallows malformed paths. Combined with the new canonicalizeURLPart call, this can mask injection attempts rather than rejecting them.
